info.title: Prontiq Private Account API
info.description: Private Clerk-authenticated account and console API contracts.

ClerkJwt.description: Clerk session token; sent as `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`.

post.summary: Recover or initialise an org envelope (Clerk JWT auth)
post.description: Idempotent. Resolves the caller's verified primary email via the Clerk Backend API and provisions the org envelope. The Clerk org id is the active Prontiq and Lago customer identity; Stripe remains only Lago's payment rail.

orgId.description: Clerk organization id and active Prontiq/Lago customer identity.

emailSent.description: True only when the welcome email was best-effort sent for a freshly-created envelope. Absent on already_exists replays.

orgId.description: Clerk organization id and active Prontiq/Lago customer identity.

emailSent.description: True only when the welcome email was best-effort sent for a freshly-created envelope. Absent on already_exists replays.

get.description: Returns the caller's org provisioning status and key-management capability. Used by the console to choose between setup recovery, first-key creation, and key-list states without probing mutation endpoints.

200.description: Account status for the active Clerk org

post.summary: Create a new API key (admin only)
post.description: Mints a fresh `pq_live_*` key, stores its hash, and returns the raw value once. The raw key is never persisted, never logged, and cannot be retrieved later. Org key limits are projected from Lago entitlements and enforced atomically.

label.description: User-supplied display label for the key. Optional. 1-64 chars.

201.description: Key created. Raw value returned once.

raw.description: The raw key. Returned ONCE in this response and never again. Treat as a credential.

keyPrefix.description: Public-safe prefix for display in masked-key tables.

get.summary: List active keys for the caller's org (member-allowed)
get.description: Returns active keys with masked prefix only. The org envelope is excluded by sentinel filter; revoked keys are excluded by the active flag.

Returns the latest API-key lifecycle audit events for the caller's org, newest first. Used by the console key-management page to show recent CREATE / ROTATE / REVOKE activity. Raw API keys, key hashes, and hash-bearing internal metadata are never present.

200.description: Recent audit events for this org

post.description: Issues a new raw key under the existing `keyId`. The old key remains valid for 5 minutes via a REDIRECT row in the usage table — clients must update credentials within that window. `createdAt` is preserved; `rotatedAt` records the rotation. Requires recent second-factor reverification (`fva` claim).

200.description: Key rotated. New raw returned once.

raw.description: The raw key for the rotated identity. Returned ONCE in this response. Old raw remains valid for 5 minutes via the REDIRECT grace.

createdAt.description: Original creation timestamp — preserved across rotation.

403.description: INSUFFICIENT_ROLE (admin gate, standard envelope) OR reverification-error (Clerk-native body for stale fva)

post.description: Marks a key as inactive. Subsequent requests with the raw key (or with the pre-rotation raw, if a rotation grace was active) return 401. Decrements `activeKeyCount` atomically so create-quota frees up. Requires recent second-factor reverification.

403.description: INSUFFICIENT_ROLE (admin gate, standard envelope) OR reverification-error (Clerk-native body for stale fva)
